channel.title: CyberNetSec - Latest Cybersecurity Articles
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity threat intelligence articles from the past 3 days

item.title: CISA Warns: Critical Adobe AEM Flaw (CVSS 10.0) Actively Exploited
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued an urgent warning for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Forms, tracked as CVE-2025-54253. The flaw, which carries a perfect 10.0 CVSS score, allows for unauthenticated arb...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Lending Platform Prosper Breached, 17.6 Million Accounts Exposed
item.description:
The peer-to-peer lending platform Prosper has confirmed a massive data breach that exposed the personal and sensitive information of approximately 17.6 million user accounts. The breach notification service 'Have I Been Pwned' has already incorporated the data set, which includes names, email addres...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK Fines Capita £14M for "Preventable" 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
The UK's Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) has levied a £14 million fine against outsourcing giant Capita for significant data protection failures related to a March 2023 data breach that impacted 6.6 million people. The ICO's investigation concluded the breach was 'preventable' and heavily cr...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Issues 13 Advisories for Critical ICS/OT Vulnerabilities
item.description:
On October 16, 2025, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) released a significant batch of thirteen advisories for vulnerabilities affecting Industrial Control Systems (ICS). These alerts impact widely used Operational Technology (OT) products from major vendors including ...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: California Enacts Stricter Data Breach Law with 30-Day Notification Deadline
item.description:
California has enacted Senate Bill 446, a new law that significantly shortens the data breach notification timeline for businesses. Organizations must now inform affected California residents of a data breach involving unencrypted personal information within 30 calendar days of its discovery. This a...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Microsoft Thwarts Ransomware Campaign by Revoking 200+ Malicious Code-Signing Certificates
item.description:
Microsoft has taken decisive action to disrupt a ransomware campaign by the threat group Vanilla Tempest (also known as Vice Society), which has been targeting education and healthcare. The group was using over 200 fraudulently obtained code-signing certificates to sign counterfeit Microsoft Teams i...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Full Industrial Control: Two CVSS 10.0 Flaws Found in Red Lion ICS RTUs
item.description:
Security researchers have discovered and disclosed two critical vulnerabilities, both rated CVSS 10.0, in Red Lion Sixnet series industrial remote terminal units (RTUs). The flaws, CVE-2023-42770 (authentication bypass) and CVE-2023-40151 (remote code execution), can be chained together. An unauthen...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'LinkPro' Linux Rootkit Uses eBPF and 'Magic Packets' for Ultimate Stealth
item.description:
Security researchers have uncovered a sophisticated new GNU/Linux rootkit named 'LinkPro' after investigating a compromised AWS environment. The malware demonstrates advanced stealth capabilities by leveraging extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF) modules to hide its processes and files from securi...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canadian Tire Reveals E-Commerce Data Breach Affecting Multiple Retail Brands
item.description:
Canadian Tire Corp., a major Canadian retail conglomerate, has reported a data breach affecting its e-commerce customers. Discovered on October 2, 2025, the incident involved unauthorized access to a single database serving multiple brands, including Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's/L'Équipeur, and ...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: LockBit Ransomware Returns from Hiatus with Upgraded 'Version 5.0'
item.description:
After a two-month hiatus following law enforcement disruption, the prolific LockBit ransomware group has returned, announcing the release of LockBit 5.0. This new version of the ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) malware incorporates significant technical upgrades designed to evade detection and analysi...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Attacks Surge by 46% as Threat Actors Target Construction and Manufacturing
item.description:
Despite a slight decrease in overall weekly cyber attacks, ransomware activity has surged by 46%, according to a new report from Check Point Research. This indicates a strategic shift by threat actors towards more focused and impactful ransomware campaigns. The construction, business services, and i...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Israeli Defense R&D Firm 'MAYA' Targeted in Pro-Resistance Hacktivist Attack
item.description:
A hacktivist group calling itself the 'Cyber Support Front' has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against MAYA, an Israeli research and development firm with close ties to the country's Ministry of Defense and major defense contractors like Elbit Systems and Rafael. In a public statement on O...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Fortinet Discloses High-Severity Authenticated RCE Flaw in FortiOS CLI
item.description:
Fortinet has disclosed a high-severity vulnerability in the command line interface (CLI) of its FortiOS operating system. The flaw could allow an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the underlying system. While a CVE identifier has not yet been assigned and specific affected vers...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →
